questions.q: How was Draupadi born?
options: From a lotus floating on the Ganga
Full-grown from her father's sacrificial fire
As an ordinary infant blessed by Shiva
From a boon granted by the sage Durvasa
questions.explanation: Draupadi rose full-grown and radiant from the sacrificial fire of King Drupada, moments after her brother Dhrishtadyumna.

questions.q: Why was Draupadi called Krishnaa?
options: Because Krishna was her dearest friend
Because she was born at midnight
Because she worshipped Krishna from childhood
questions.explanation: The name Krishnaa means the dark one, given for her dark and dazzling complexion at birth.

questions.q: How many husbands did Draupadi have?
questions.explanation: Draupadi was the shared wife of all five Pandava brothers, a marriage sanctioned by Vyasa and her own past-life boon.

questions.q: Who dragged Draupadi by the hair into the Kuru assembly after the dice game?
questions.explanation: Dushasana hauled Draupadi into the court by her hair at Duryodhana's command, an outrage Bhima vowed to avenge.

questions.q: Who was Draupadi's father?
questions.explanation: Draupadi was the daughter of Drupada, king of Panchala, which is the source of her name.

questions.q: From what does the name Panchali derive?
options: Her being a princess of Panchala
The five fires of her birth rite
questions.explanation: Panchali simply means the woman of Panchala, her father Drupada's kingdom.

questions.q: Who is credited in the most retold version with making Draupadi's sari endless during the disrobing?
questions.explanation: Popular retellings credit Krishna's grace with the endless cloth, while the critical edition attributes the miracle to unseen dharma.

questions.q: Which brother of Draupadi emerged from the same sacrificial fire?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna stepped armed from the flames just before Draupadi rose from the altar.

questions.q: What name did Draupadi take while in disguise during the thirteenth year of exile?
questions.explanation: Draupadi served as Sairandhri, a maid skilled in dressing hair, in the household of King Virata.

questions.q: Whom did Draupadi serve as a maid during the year in hiding?
questions.explanation: Draupadi attended Sudeshna, Virata's queen, whose brother Kichaka would fatally desire her.

questions.q: What question did Draupadi put to the assembly after the dice game?
options: Whether Shakuni's dice had been honest
Whether a king could wager his own brothers
Whether Yudhishthira, having first lost himself, had any right to stake her
Whether Bhishma would grant her his protection
questions.explanation: Her question, whether a man already lost could stake his wife, silenced the elders, and no one in the hall could answer it.

questions.q: According to popular tradition, what vow did Draupadi take concerning her hair after the dice hall?
options: To shave it off until her husbands were avenged
To braid it with iron threads of mourning
To cover it before every king but her husbands
To leave it unbound until it was washed with Dushasana's blood
questions.explanation: The vow of unbound hair is a famous later tradition built on Bhima's blood oath in the epic, and it ended only after Dushasana's death.

questions.q: What did Draupadi ask for with the boons Dhritarashtra offered her?
options: Freedom for Yudhishthira, then freedom with weapons for his brothers
The return of her jewels and her father's lands
Banishment of Shakuni and Dushasana from court
A promise that no dice game would ever be played again
questions.explanation: With her first boon she freed Yudhishthira from slavery and with her second the other four Pandavas along with their weapons.

questions.q: Why did Draupadi decline a third boon from Dhritarashtra?
options: She feared the old king would trick her
She said greed was unbecoming, as two boons sufficed for a kshatriya woman
Bhishma forbade her from asking for more
Her husbands signaled her to ask nothing further
questions.explanation: Draupadi answered that covetousness destroys virtue and that her husbands, once free, could win back everything themselves.

questions.q: Which king abducted Draupadi from the hermitage during the forest exile?
questions.explanation: Jayadratha of Sindhu carried Draupadi off from the Kamyaka forest and was caught and humiliated by the Pandavas, his head shaved in disgrace.

questions.q: Who harassed Draupadi at Virata's court and was crushed by Bhima in the dance hall?
questions.explanation: Kichaka, the queen's brother and commander of Virata's forces, pursued Sairandhri and was lured to a midnight tryst where Bhima killed him.

questions.q: By what collective name were Draupadi's five sons known?
questions.explanation: Her five sons, one by each Pandava, were called the Upapandavas and were slain in Ashwatthama's night raid.

questions.q: From what does Draupadi's name Yajnaseni derive?
options: Her birth during a war sacrifice
Her lifelong devotion to fire rituals
The sage who blessed her at birth
questions.explanation: Drupada was also called Yajnasena, so his fire-born daughter bore the name Yajnaseni.

questions.q: Who laid down the rule governing Draupadi's time with each husband, with exile for any intruder?
questions.explanation: Narada, citing the brothers Sunda and Upasunda who died over a woman, had the Pandavas agree to a compact about Draupadi's company.

questions.q: Which husband broke the privacy compact and accepted a long pilgrimage as penance?
questions.explanation: Arjuna entered the chamber to fetch weapons for a brahmin's cause and honored the penalty with a twelve-year pilgrimage.

questions.q: Whom did Draupadi call upon when Durvasa and his disciples arrived after she had already eaten?
questions.explanation: Krishna answered her plea, ate the single grain left in the akshaya patra, and the hunger of all the guests was satisfied.

questions.q: Of which goddess was Draupadi held to be the earthly incarnation?
questions.explanation: The epic's roll of divine descents names Draupadi as the incarnation of Shri, born to be the wife of the five Pandavas.

questions.q: What did a divine voice proclaim at Draupadi's birth?
options: That she would bear a hundred sons
That she would one day reign in Indra's heaven
That the dark beauty would bring about the destruction of the kshatriyas
That she would never know sorrow in her life
questions.explanation: The voice declared that Krishnaa, best of women, would accomplish the purpose of the gods and lead the kshatriyas to ruin.

questions.q: Which of Draupadi's sons was fathered by Yudhishthira?
questions.explanation: Prativindhya was her son by Yudhishthira; Sutasoma, Shrutakarma, Shatanika, and Shrutasena were by the other four.

questions.q: After the night raid killed her sons, what did Draupadi demand as the price of Ashwatthama's life?
options: The gem set in his forehead
His right hand that held the blade
questions.explanation: Draupadi asked for the jewel born in Ashwatthama's brow, which was prised out and placed on Yudhishthira's head.

questions.q: Why did Draupadi ultimately spare Ashwatthama's life?
options: Krishna commanded her to be silent
She would not have his mother weep as she wept, and he was their guru's son
Yudhishthira had sworn an oath of protection
She feared the sin of slaying an immortal
questions.explanation: Even in grief Draupadi refused to make Kripi mourn a son as she mourned hers, and held the guru's son beyond killing.

questions.q: With which queen did Draupadi discuss the duties of a devoted wife during the forest exile?
questions.explanation: In the celebrated forest dialogue, Draupadi explained to Krishna's wife Satyabhama how she served her husbands and managed a royal household.

questions.q: On the final journey north, what reason did Yudhishthira give for Draupadi falling first?
options: Her vanity in her unmatched beauty
Her curse upon the Kuru elders
Her partiality toward Arjuna over her other husbands
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira said that though wed to five, her heart favored Arjuna, and that partiality brought her down on the great journey.

questions.q: In the critical edition of the epic, how is Draupadi's protection during the disrobing described?
options: Robes appear endlessly through unseen dharma, without Krishna being named
Krishna arrives in person and shields her in the hall
Gandhari rushes in and covers her with her own veil
Indra sends down celestial garments from heaven
questions.explanation: In the critical edition garment after garment simply appears as Dushasana pulls, a miracle of dharma; the appeal to Krishna is a later expansion.

questions.q: What condition was Draupadi in when summoned to the dice hall, deepening the outrage?
options: She was fasting under a vow of silence
She was in her courses, clad in a single garment
She was in mourning white for her father
She was carrying her firstborn son
questions.explanation: Draupadi protested that she was in her period and wearing a single cloth, making her dragging into the full court doubly shameful.
